questions.question: Why is Diffie-Hellman considered a key agreement protocol rather than an encryption algorithm? (2 marks)
keyPoints: It does not encrypt data directly/payload
It only establishes a shared secret key
The established key is then used for symmetric encryption

questions.question: Explain, with the aid of a diagram, the Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange process. Discuss its major vulnerability and how it can be mitigated. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Diagram showing Alice/Bob, public parameters (p,g), private keys (a,b), public keys (A,B), shared secret (S) calculation
Explanation of each step and the final matching shared secret
Statement of vulnerability: Man-in-the-Middle attack due to lack of authentication
Mitigation method: Using digital signatures or digital certificates to authenticate the public keys
